objective: Add or collect NVTX/Nsight Systems evidence for the accepted C++ MMAPI/VPI/NVENC device-side stage, so the final project can explain decode, transform, wrapper, submit/sync, warp, encode, and wall-time boundaries with a timeline.

design_context.refined_project_positioning: Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization, with EIS as the representative real-time vision workload.
design_context.accepted_device_path: MMAPI/NVDEC block-linear NV12 main chain -> NvBufSurfTransform to pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch -> VPI CUDA PerspectiveWarp -> NvBufSurfTransform back to block-linear NV12 -> NVENC
design_context.known_stage_boundary: wrapper lifecycle, VPI sync, and NvBufSurfTransform sandwich dominate more than vpiSubmitPerspectiveWarp itself.
design_context.rk3588_idea_mapping: The RK3588 NPU reference is useful as a methodology: separate hardware time from wall-clock time, detect host scheduling starvation, and only then consider C++ shared-buffer or multi-in-flight dataflow. RKNN core-mask and rknn_create_mem APIs do not transfer directly.

profiling_questions: Is VPI/CUDA/NVENC idle while CPU-side wrapper, transform, or sync work is happening?
Is the measured wall-clock gap dominated by host scheduling rather than VPI PerspectiveWarp execution?
Would queue depth, double buffering, or multi-in-flight submission plausibly reduce idle gaps?
Does format-matched NvBuffer pair reduce only wrapper overhead, or does it also change sync/transform timing?
Are any proposed optimizations changing output semantics, matrix semantics, or crop/postprocess boundaries?
frozen_variables: matrix: resid_r15_s07 for quality-preserving comparisons, or a clearly labeled diagnostic matrix for pure dataflow probes
matrix convention: source_to_dest for Regular gate quality work
consumer path: accepted C++ MMAPI/VPI/NVENC path or the format-matched NvBuffer pair path
no algorithm quality tuning inside this contract
no change from block-linear main chain to pitch-linear encoder main path
external_observation: Nsight Systems report or exported summary exists
stage names are visible or recoverable from NVTX ranges/logs
no new zero-copy or full real-time claim is introduced
profiling distinguishes hardware execution time from wall-clock and host-side wait time
success_criteria: timeline or exported summary separates decode, input transform, wrapper or NvBuffer wrap, VPI submit, VPI sync, output transform, encode, and wall time
result table can explain why the bottleneck is dataflow/sync/wrapper rather than the PerspectiveWarp API call alone
claim boundary stays module/dataflow-level, not full EIS pipeline acceleration
evidence path is recorded in README/presentation/long-term context if produced

recommended_steps.title: Instrumentation audit and RK3588 idea mapping
recommended_steps.objective: Inspect the accepted C++ sample, existing timing probes, and docs/rk3588_npu_idea_mapping_2026-07-23.md to decide which stage markers are required.

recommended_steps.title: NVTX range insertion or Nsight capture
recommended_steps.objective: Add minimal stage ranges or run Nsight Systems against the existing stage without changing output semantics. The capture should answer whether hardware is starved by host-side wrapper/sync/transform scheduling.

recommended_steps.title: Timeline export and summary
recommended_steps.objective: Produce one timeline screenshot/export and one concise table mapping stage cost to engineering interpretation.

recommended_steps.title: Route decision and presentation update
recommended_steps.objective: If the timeline shows idle gaps, propose a separate same-source dataflow A/B contract for queue depth or double buffering. Otherwise record the RK3588 idea as a methodology reference and update final wording with measured Nsight evidence.

forbidden_claims: full-pipeline acceleration from module-only or stage-only evidence
stop_reasons: Jetson SSH or Nsight runtime blocker
Nsight capture requires unavailable GUI/manual action
would require broad C++ rewrite instead of minimal profiling instrumentation
